reasoning_summary: Run 20260421_185050_bf16_gemm_v1_434ded2a at 25.461216 ms confirms that the round-6 regression was mostly a register-budget mistake: registers dropped from 254 to 202, barrier dropped from 6.72% to 5.76%, and mio_throttle dropped from 5.80% to 3.81%. But the grouped_rows=2 retest still failed to beat the known PTX anchor because long_scoreboard snapped back from 0.12% to 6.10% and runtime stayed 0.40 ms slower than the earlier two-stage grouped_rows=4 result. That makes grouped_rows restoration the clearest next move. I rank a direct grouped_rows 2 -> 4 restore on the current compact two-stage ring first because it is the smallest correction that directly targets the newly dominant long_scoreboard rebound. I keep a compact two-stage barrier-cadence trim second because, once grouped_rows is back at 4, the remaining local tax should return to CTA handoff overhead on the 22,016 B shared-memory surface. I keep the 256x128 / 64x64 structural reopen third because the persistent human guidance still points there as the main higher-ceiling branch after the local PTX deconfounding work.
family_audit: accept: the compact two-stage reset was the right recovery move because it removed the round-6 register explosion and restored the 22,016 B shared-memory footprint.
accept: the grouped_rows=2 retest is now deconfounded and looks negative on the compact two-stage surface because long_scoreboard rebounded to 6.10% while runtime stayed above the known anchor.
defer_but_keep_open: preserve both the compact two-stage barrier-cadence trim and the 256x128 / 64x64 structural reopen after grouped_rows is restored.

directions.name: Restore Grouped Rows From 2 Back To 4 On The Compact Two-Stage PTX Ring
directions.hypothesis: The grouped_rows=2 retest kept the recovered register budget but clearly reintroduced the long_scoreboard problem on the compact two-stage surface. Restoring grouped_rows to 4 should recover the better row-order reuse pattern from the earlier PTX anchor without reopening the losing round-6 split logic.
directions.expected_bottleneck: Long-scoreboard pressure from the grouped_rows=2 launch order is now the most specific local bottleneck to remove.
directions.risk: Low. This is a single-parameter launch-order restore on the currently healthy compact two-stage PTX surface.
ranking_notes: This is the highest-confidence next move because it directly targets the one metric that snapped back hardest on the compact two-stage surface: long_scoreboard.
It preserves the recovered 202-register budget and 22,016 B shared-memory footprint instead of reopening broader pipeline surgery.
If it still cannot beat the current 25.46 ms accepted base, the compact PTX family is likely plateaued and the next branch should be either barrier trim or 256x128.
directions.stop_condition: Stop if restoring grouped_rows to 4 requires any change beyond the hot-band launch-order constant and its immediate grouping math.
expected_local_changes: Leave the compact two-stage PTX ring unchanged.
Re-measure whether the long_scoreboard rebound disappears without giving back the recovered register budget.

directions.name: Trim The Compact Two-Stage PTX Wait-Sync Cadence Without Growing Shared Memory
directions.hypothesis: Once grouped_rows is restored, the remaining local gap on the compact 22,016 B PTX surface should again be CTA handoff overhead rather than launch-order damage. A narrow wait/sync cadence trim on the compact two-stage ring could reduce barrier stalls without reintroducing the shared-memory or register blowups seen in the larger stage experiments.
directions.expected_bottleneck: Barrier and CTA handoff overhead are the likely next bottlenecks after grouped_rows is restored.
directions.risk: Moderate. This is still hot-loop PTX synchronization surgery, but it stays on the compact 22,016 B shared-memory surface rather than reopening the failed staged branch.
ranking_notes: This is the best local follow-up once grouped_rows is back at 4 because it attacks the remaining sync tax without changing stage depth.
It avoids the 30,464 B shared-memory blowup and 254-register failure mode from the round-6 split.
It stays below dir_01 because the current run is still more specifically explained by launch-order damage than by residual barrier cost.
directions.stop_condition: Stop if the edit requires increasing shared memory above 22,016 B or materially increasing registers beyond the current 202-register budget.
expected_local_changes: Retune the compact two-stage wait_group and __syncthreads cadence without changing stage depth.
Keep the PTX microkernel on the 22,016 B shared-memory budget.
Do not change grouped_rows again in the same implementation.

directions.name: Reopen The 256x128 64x64-Warp Hot-Band Branch From The Compact PTX Base
directions.hypothesis: If the compact PTX branch remains stuck near 25 ms even after grouped_rows is restored, the geometry ceiling is probably real. The repo already contains a writer-safe 256x128 / 64x64 path aligned with the persistent human guidance, so that structural branch should remain on deck.
directions.expected_bottleneck: The 128x128 PTX surface may still be occupancy-limited by geometry and warp reuse even after local launch-order cleanup.
directions.risk: High. This is a broader structural reopen that touches geometry, ownership, and export path together.
ranking_notes: This remains the highest-ceiling structural branch after the local PTX launch-order work is exhausted.
The writer-safe 256x128 path is already present in the tree, so the reopen is bounded even though it is still risky.
It stays rank 3 because the immediate evidence still points to a smaller launch-order correction first.
directions.stop_condition: Stop if the reopen requires reviving a previously broken ownership or export path instead of the writer-safe 256x128 surface already present in the source.
expected_local_changes: Re-enable the writer-safe 256x128 / 64x64 PTX hot-band branch.
Keep async-copy staging and shared-memory reuse intact on the larger CTA geometry.
Leave non-hot-band routing unchanged in the first pass.

notes: Round 8 diagnosis emitted after the compact two-stage grouped_rows=2 retest recovered registers but reintroduced a large long_scoreboard penalty.
